---
title: "GRUB timeout not working !"

forum: General Help

### Post by ranjith19 on 2010-11-08
I am running Ubuntu 10.10. Grub timeout is not working and I have to press enter every time I switch on the computer after the Grub menu loads. Here is my /boot/grub/grub.cfg file


[FONT="Courier New"]#
# DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE
#
# It is automatically generated by grub-mkconfig using templates
# from /etc/grub.d and settings from /etc/default/grub
#

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/00_header ###
if [ -s $prefix/grubenv ]; then
  load_env
fi
set default="0"
if [ ${prev_saved_entry} ]; then
  set saved_entry=${prev_saved_entry}
  save_env saved_entry
  set prev_saved_entry=
  save_env prev_saved_entry
  set boot_once=true
fi

function savedefault {
  if [ -z ${boot_once} ]; then
    saved_entry=${chosen}
    save_env saved_entry
  fi
}

function recordfail {
  set recordfail=1
  if [ -n ${have_grubenv} ]; then if [ -z ${boot_once} ]; then save_env recordfail; fi; fi
}
insmod part_msdos
insmod ext2
set root='(hd0,msdos9)'
search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 203caa1b-04c1-490e-8ff2-dfdaac913c68
if loadfont /usr/share/grub/unicode.pf2 ; then
set gfxmode=1024x768
  insmod gfxterm
  insmod 
  if terminal_output gfxterm ; then true ; else
    # For backward compatibility with versions of terminal.mod that don't
    # understand terminal_output
    terminal gfxterm
  fi
fi
insmod part_msdos
insmod ext2
set root='(hd0,msdos9)'
search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 203caa1b-04c1-490e-8ff2-dfdaac913c68
set locale_dir=($root)/boot/grub/locale
set lang=en
insmod gettext
if [ ${recordfail} = 1 ]; then
  set timeout=-1
else
  set timeout=4
fi
### END /etc/grub.d/00_header ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/05_debian_theme ###
set menu_color_normal=white/black
set menu_color_highlight=black/light-gray
### END /etc/grub.d/05_debian_theme ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/10_linux ###
menuentry 'Ubuntu, with Linux 2.6.35-22-generic' --class ubuntu --class gnu-linux --class gnu --class os {
	recordfail
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd0,msdos9)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 203caa1b-04c1-490e-8ff2-dfdaac913c68
	linux	/boot/vmlinuz-2.6.35-22-generic root=UUID=203caa1b-04c1-490e-8ff2-dfdaac913c68 ro  vga=769  quiet splash nomodeset video=uvesafb:mode_option=1280x800-24,mtrr=3,scroll=ywrap
	initrd	/boot/initrd.img-2.6.35-22-generic
}
menuentry 'Ubuntu, with Linux 2.6.35-22-generic (recovery mode)' --class ubuntu --class gnu-linux --class gnu --class os {
	recordfail
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd0,msdos9)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 203caa1b-04c1-490e-8ff2-dfdaac913c68
	echo	'Loading Linux 2.6.35-22-generic ...'
	linux	/boot/vmlinuz-2.6.35-22-generic root=UUID=203caa1b-04c1-490e-8ff2-dfdaac913c68 ro single  vga=769
	echo	'Loading initial ramdisk ...'
	initrd	/boot/initrd.img-2.6.35-22-generic
}
### END /etc/grub.d/10_linux ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/20_linux_xen ###
### END /etc/grub.d/20_linux_xen ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/20_memtest86+ ###
menuentry "Memory test (memtest86+)" {
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd0,msdos9)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 203caa1b-04c1-490e-8ff2-dfdaac913c68
	linux16	/boot/memtest86+.bin
}
menuentry "Memory test (memtest86+, serial console 115200)" {
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd0,msdos9)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 203caa1b-04c1-490e-8ff2-dfdaac913c68
	linux16	/boot/memtest86+.bin console=ttyS0,115200n8
}
### END /etc/grub.d/20_memtest86+ ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/30_os-prober ###
menuentry "Windows 7 (loader) (on /dev/sda1)" {
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ntfs
	set root='(hd0,msdos1)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 9cdaf875daf84cd0
	chainloader +1
}
### END /etc/grub.d/30_os-prober ###

  source $prefix/custom.cfg;
fi
### END /etc/grub.d/41_custom ###[/FONT]



Please help.

### Post by oldfred on 2010-11-08
Some info may be here:

### Post by ranjith19 on 2010-11-08
I do not want the hidden timeout to work. The timeout after the menu is shown is not working.

---

### Post by efflandt on 2010-11-08
Do NOT edit /boot/grub/grub.cfg

What do you have in the first section of your **/etc/default/grub**?  That is what sets time outs.  The following line should be commented out (leading #):

#GRUB_HIDDEN_TIMEOUT=0

and the following line sets the menu timeout:

GRUB_TIMEOUT=10

See "Configuring GRUB2" section of [https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Grub2](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Grub2)

---
